sops.title: Call Opening and Patient Identification
steps: Greet caller: 'Thank you for calling Grace Health System. My name is [Agent Name]. How can I assist you today?'
If patient: Collect first and last name, date of birth (DOB), and verify last 4 of SSN or account number

If caller is not the patient (family member, caregiver): Verify they are listed as an authorized representative in the patient's record. If not listed, limit to general information only — do NOT share PHI.
Confirm correct patient record is pulled before proceeding
Note language preference and initiate interpreter service if needed
sops.hipaa_note: Never confirm or deny whether an individual is a patient to an unverified caller.

sops.title: Handling Angry or Distressed Callers
steps: Remain calm and professional at all times
Acknowledge the patient's frustration: 'I understand this is frustrating and I want to help resolve this for you.'
Do not argue, interrupt, or match the caller's tone
If the caller uses abusive or threatening language, issue one warning: 'I want to help you, but I need us to speak respectfully. If the language continues, I will need to end this call.'
If abusive language continues: 'I'm going to need to end this call. Please call back when you're ready and we will be happy to assist you.' Then disconnect.
Document the interaction in the call log with timestamps

sops.title: Appointment Scheduling Workflow
steps: Confirm patient identity (see SOP-CC-001)
Ask: 'What is the reason for your visit today?' — use triage protocol to determine urgency
For emergent symptoms: redirect to 911 or ED immediately
For urgent: offer same-day or next-day appointment
For routine: offer next available in 3–7 days
Ask for preferred location, provider (if established), and telehealth vs. in-person preference
Confirm insurance is active and accepted — advise patient to bring card
Confirm appointment date, time, provider, and location — read back to patient
Send confirmation via MyGrace portal, text (if opted in), or mail

sops.title: Prescription Refill Request
steps: Verify patient identity
Collect medication name, dose, and prescribing provider
Confirm this is NOT a controlled substance (Schedule II–V)
If non-controlled: submit refill request to provider via EHR task — advise 48–72 hour processing
If controlled substance: explain new prescription is required and schedule appointment
If patient is running out in less than 72 hours of a critical medication: flag as URGENT to on-call provider
Do not make clinical recommendations (e.g., dose changes, switching medications) — refer to clinical staff

sops.title: Test Results Inquiry
steps: Verify patient identity
Confirm the type of test and approximate date
Check if results are available in MyGrace patient portal
If results are released in portal: direct patient to portal and offer to walk through access
If results are NOT yet released: inform patient that providers review results before releasing — advise to check portal within 3–5 business days or the provider will call
NEVER interpret lab values or imaging results — refer to provider or nurse
If patient reports a critical symptom after receiving results: triage immediately (see triage protocol)

sops.title: Billing and Insurance Inquiries
steps: For general billing questions: provide Billing Department number (555-400-9000) and hours
For financial hardship inquiries: provide Financial Counseling line (555-400-9010) and mention charity care options
Do not discuss specific account balances unless patient is verified and question is basic
For disputes, payment plan setup, or itemized bill requests: transfer to Billing Department
For insurance verification questions: advise patient to contact their insurer directly or call Grace Health prior to visit

sops.title: Post-Discharge Follow-Up Calls
steps: Identify yourself and Grace Health System
Confirm patient was recently discharged from Grace Health
Ask: 'How are you feeling since your discharge?'
Review discharge instructions were received and understood
Confirm follow-up appointment is scheduled — if not, schedule now
Ask about new or worsening symptoms
Confirm medications were filled and patient knows how to take them
Flag any red-flag symptoms to the care coordinator or on-call nurse
Document outcome in EHR

sops.title: AI Agent Escalation Triggers
steps: AI agent must transfer to human immediately when:
— Patient reports emergency symptoms (chest pain, stroke, uncontrolled bleeding, etc.)
— Patient expresses suicidal ideation or is in mental health crisis
— Patient or caller becomes abusive or threatening
— Patient is requesting clinical advice beyond scheduling/FAQs
— Patient is confused, disoriented, or appears to be in distress
— Patient requests to speak with a human three or more times
— Patient's concern cannot be resolved within the AI's knowledge base
— Caller is a minor calling alone about a health concern
Upon transfer: AI must summarize caller name, DOB, reason for call, and any triage information to the receiving agent before full handoff
